description: Wave optics explains interference and diffraction through the Huygens-Fresnel principle: each point on a wavefront emits secondary wavelets whose amplitudes superpose, and the path difference between contributions decides constructive versus destructive interference, producing the characteristic fringe intensity pattern set by wavelength and aperture geometry.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Coherent light source"]
    N2["Huygens-Fresnel wavelets"]
    N3["Superpose amplitudes"]
    N4["Path difference delta"]
    N5{"Constructive or destructive?"}
    N6["delta = m*lambda: bright"]
    N7["Half-integer: dark fringe"]
    N8["Build fringe pattern"]
    N9["Aperture / slit width"]
    N10["Intensity pattern I(theta)"]
